businessSummary.oneLiner: Leading AI data analytics platform serving government and commercial sectors through Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI-powered data analytics platforms for government and enterprise clients. FY2025 revenue hit $4.48B (+56% YoY), with US commercial revenue crossing $500M for the first time, marking a pivotal shift toward commercial diversification. AIPCon 9 yielded major partnerships with NVIDIA and GE Aerospace. Notable concern: CEO Karp and insiders have been selling approximately $6M of shares daily in 2026, though these appear to be pre-planned 10b5-1 transactions.

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Over 10x the S&P 500 average of 22x. Significantly above the software sector average of 35x

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS estimate of $1.34. Growth premium is steep but decelerating rapidly

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 $4.48B, FY2026E $7.41B (+65.5%). Growth is actually accelerating

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Top-tier among SaaS companies. Achieving high profitability alongside rapid growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: $2.1B FCF. Best-in-class cash generation among software peers

keyMetrics.name: Price-to-Book
keyMetrics.interpretation: $7.18B cash on hand. Extreme PBR reflects intangible asset value

growthDrivers.title: AIPCon 9 Major Partnerships
growthDrivers.description: Strategic partnerships with NVIDIA, GE Aerospace, and other global enterprises. Positioning as a core player in the AI agent ecosystem

growthDrivers.title: NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS Reference Architecture
growthDrivers.description: Palantir software deployed as the standard OS on NVIDIA hardware for sovereign AI infrastructure across nations

growthDrivers.title: US Army $10B + Anduril $20B Subcontractor Upside
growthDrivers.description: Direct $10B Army contract plus potential subcontracting on Anduril's $20B Army contract. Defense pipeline expanding significantly

growthDrivers.title: GE Aerospace Military Aircraft AI Maintenance
growthDrivers.description: AI-powered predictive maintenance collaboration with GE Aerospace for military aircraft fleets

growthDrivers.title: Government AI Spending Expansion + AIP Bootcamps
growthDrivers.description: Rising US government AI budgets combined with high-conversion AIP Bootcamp (5-day intensive pilot) strategy for enterprise customers

growthDrivers.title: FY2026 Estimates Substantially Raised
growthDrivers.description: FY2026E revenue $7.41B (+65.5%), EPS $1.34 (+113%). Revenue growth is accelerating, not decelerating

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government and defense AI deployment. Security clearance requirements create formidable barriers to entry. NVIDIA Sovereign AI partnership positions Palantir at the center of the AI agent ecosystem.

moats.description: Highest-level security clearances with US DoD and intelligence agencies. Virtually impossible for competitors to replicate.
Sovereign AI OS running on NVIDIA hardware. Positioned as the default infrastructure for government AI deployments globally.
5-day intensive pilots that convert enterprise prospects into full platform deployments at high conversion rates.

competitors.name: Microsoft (Azure AI)
competitors.detail: Cloud AI platform with broad enterprise reach. Generalist approach vs. Palantir's specialized analytics

competitors.name: Snowflake
competitors.detail: Data lakehouse expanding into analytics platform territory

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform. Trails Palantir significantly in scale and proven deployments

recentNews.headline: Insider selling continues: Karp and insiders selling ~$6M of PLTR shares daily in 2026
recentNews.significance: CEO Karp sold 493,025 shares on 2/20 at ~$144.85. Pre-planned 10b5-1 sales but the scale is notable

recentNews.headline: Seeking Alpha upgrades PLTR from Hold to Buy
recentNews.significance: Upgrade driven by aggressive growth investment and accelerating revenue trajectory

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: Key PLTR earnings detail was US commercial revenue passing $500M
recentNews.significance: Milestone signals successful commercial diversification beyond government dependence

recentNews.headline: Dan Ives (Wedbush) reaffirms $230 price target post-AIPCon 9
recentNews.significance: Leading AI sector analyst maintains 48% upside target from current levels

recentNews.headline: Anduril secures $20B US Army contract; PLTR positioned as subcontractor
recentNews.significance: Palantir expected to participate as data analytics subcontractor, expanding defense revenue pipeline

analystOpinions.notableComment: Wedbush Dan Ives reaffirms $230 target. MarketBeat consensus $192.68. 16 of 28 analysts rate Buy. UBS upgrades to Strong Buy.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation
risks.description: TTM P/E of 225x is 10x the S&P 500 average. Any deceleration in growth could trigger severe multiple compression

risks.title: AI Model Supply Chain Risk
risks.description: Potential restrictions on AI model access (e.g., Anthropic/Claude blacklist) could weaken platform competitiveness

risks.title: Persistent Insider Selling
risks.description: CEO Karp and insiders selling approximately $6M daily in 2026. Pre-planned 10b5-1 sales, but the optics raise questions about management confidence

risks.title: Government Revenue Concentration
risks.description: Significant revenue dependence on US government contracts. Administration changes or budget cuts pose direct risk

risks.title: Share Dilution
risks.description: Stock-based compensation dilutes shareholders by approximately 5% annually

risks.title: Intensifying AI Market Competition
risks.description: Microsoft, AWS, and Google are ramping enterprise AI investments. Long-term market share defense required

buyReasons.title: AI Software Market Leader
buyReasons.rationale: Dominant position in government and enterprise AI analytics with 20+ years of battle-tested deployments

buyReasons.title: AIPCon 9 Strategic Partnerships
buyReasons.rationale: NVIDIA, GE Aerospace, and other tier-1 partnerships provide growth accelerants across verticals

buyReasons.title: Defense Revenue Pipeline $10B+
buyReasons.rationale: Direct $10B Army contract plus Anduril $20B subcontracting creates one of the deepest defense pipelines in software

buyReasons.title: Rapid Valuation Normalization Potential
buyReasons.rationale: FY2026E revenue +65.5% and EPS +113% growth means Forward PE compresses rapidly if estimates hold

buyReasons.title: Superior FCF and Reinvestment Capacity
buyReasons.rationale: 47% FCF margin with $7.18B cash provides ample runway for M&A and organic expansion

buyReasons.title: Sovereign AI Expands TAM
buyReasons.rationale: NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS opens global government AI infrastructure market as a new revenue stream

overallOpinion: Palantir is the clear leader in AI software, and revenue growth is accelerating from 56% to 65%. US commercial revenue crossing $500M is a major milestone that reduces government dependence.

The challenge is the 225x P/E price tag. No matter how fast the growth, this valuation leaves zero margin for error. CEO Karp selling $6M daily is worth monitoring, but these are pre-planned 10b5-1 sales, so avoid overreaction.

Trading just below the 50-day SMA at $158 with RSI at 48, it sits in a neutral zone. Down 25% from the $207 high, this is not a capitulation level, but the valuation argues against aggressive buying. Dollar-cost averaging is the prudent approach.

Anduril's $20B subcontracting upside and the NVIDIA Sovereign AI partnership underpin the medium-term growth story. Accept short-term volatility, but this is not a name you want to miss entirely during a growth acceleration phase.

investmentVerdict.summary: The growth thesis is compelling, but the valuation is extreme. A dollar-cost averaging strategy on pullbacks is appropriate. Favor entries in the $140-150 range over chasing at current levels.

threads.hook: Palantir's CEO is selling $6M of stock daily. Here's why analysts still say Buy.
threads.text:
PLTR $155.08 (market cap $370.9B)

CEO Karp has been selling roughly $6M of shares daily in 2026. Pre-planned 10b5-1 transactions, but the scale is hard to ignore.

Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha just upgraded to Buy. The catalyst? US commercial revenue crossed $500M for the first time. That's the real pivot away from government dependence.

225x trailing P/E is extreme, but revenue growth is accelerating from 56% to 65%. FCF margin sits at 47% with $7.18B cash.

Anduril's $20B Army contract creates subcontracting upside. NVIDIA Sovereign AI partnership expands TAM globally.

Consensus target $192.68 (24% upside). Dan Ives holds at $230.

The growth story is intact. DCA on pullbacks near $140-150.

fact.hook: Palantir $155: Insider selling and revenue acceleration happening simultaneously
fact.text:
PLTR $155.08 (market cap $370.9B)

CEO Karp selling approximately $6M of shares daily in 2026 via pre-planned 10b5-1 sales. Simultaneously, Seeking Alpha upgrades to Buy.

US commercial revenue crosses $500M. FY2025 revenue $4.48B (+56%), FY2026E $7.41B (+65.5%).

Anduril $20B Army contract subcontractor upside. NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS partnership.

P/E 225x, FCF margin 47%, cash $7.18B. Consensus target $192.68 (24% upside).

DCA on pullbacks is the appropriate strategy.

witty.hook: Palantir CEO daily routine: run an AI empire, sell $6M of stock, repeat
witty.text:
PLTR $155

The CEO sells $6M of stock every single day and the company is having its best year ever. Peak Silicon Valley.

Revenue +56%, accelerating to +65%. US commercial revenue finally cleared $500M.

Anduril's $20B Army deal means more subcontracting work flowing in.

225x PE makes a restaurant wine list look reasonable, but this growth means next year's PE gets cut in half.

Consensus says $192 (24% upside). Dan Ives is chilling at $230.

Conclusion: The CEO can sell all he wants. The business is buying itself. Wait for $140 and join the party.

empathy.hook: To Palantir shareholders: here's how to think about the insider selling news
empathy.text:
PLTR $155

CEO Karp selling $6M of stock daily made headlines. If you're holding, feeling uneasy is completely normal.

These are 10b5-1 pre-scheduled sales, separate from his views on the company's direction.

Meanwhile, US commercial revenue crossed $500M for the first time. Revenue growth is accelerating from 56% to 65%.

225x P/E is a lot to stomach, but at this growth rate, the multiple normalizes faster than you'd think.

If you're a long-term holder, focus on quarterly results rather than daily headlines. If you're looking to enter, dollar-cost averaging near $140-150 reduces the anxiety.
